stories.title: Fery Into Wimbledon Semifinals — British Wild Card Makes History

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Arthur Fery — British wildcard, world No. 114 — defeats No. 9 Cobolli 6-4, 7-6(4), 6-0. He is in the Wimbledon semifinals. The second wildcard to do this in the Open Era. The first was Ivanisevic in 2001. He won the title. #Wimbledon #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Arthur Fery is the 5th British man to reach the Wimbledon SF in the Open Era — after Taylor, Henman, Murray, and Norrie. He is 23. He is ranked No. 114. And he did not drop a set in the third. 6-0 in the decider. #Wimbledon #Fery #TennisFanrecap

research.facts:
Arthur Fery Into Wimbledon Semifinals
**Sources:** atptour.com, espn.com, sportico.com, olympics.com
**Key Facts (confidence-tagged):**
- [HIGH] Arthur Fery defeated No. 9 Flavio Cobolli 6-4, 7-6(4), 6-0 on Day 10, Wednesday July 8 — espn.com, atptour.com
- [HIGH] Fery is ranked world No. 114 — atptour.com
- [HIGH] Fery is a British wildcard — multiple sources
- [HIGH] Second men's wildcard to reach Wimbledon SF in the Open Era — espn.com, sportico.com
- [HIGH] First wildcard to do so was Goran Ivanisevic (No. 125) who won the title in 2001 — sportico.com, atptour.com
- [HIGH] Lowest-ranked men's Wimbledon semifinalist since Ivanisevic (No. 125) in 2001 — espn.com, sportico.com
- [HIGH] Fifth British man in Wimbledon SF in Open Era (after Roger Taylor, Tim Henman, Andy Murray, Cameron Norrie) — espn.com
- [HIGH] Fery's father, Loic Fery, is president of FC Lorient (Ligue 1), hedge fund manager — sportico.com
- [HIGH] Fery had six tour-level wins before this tournament — espn.com
- [HIGH] Had played three previous Wimbledons as wildcard, never past Round 2 — sportico.com
- [HIGH] Faces Alexander Zverev in Friday semifinal — atptour.com
**Story angle:** Historic British underdog story — wildcard with six career wins might win Wimbledon. Ivanisevic parallel.

stories.title: Zverev Ends Fritz Streak, Reaches First Wimbledon Semifinal — Paris-London Double Alive

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Alexander Zverev defeats Taylor Fritz 6-4, 6-4, 6-2. First Wimbledon SF in his career. He was 0-7 against Fritz going into today. He won in under two hours. The Paris-London double is now alive. #Wimbledon #Zverev #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Zverev won Roland Garros. Now he is in the Wimbledon semifinals. The last man to win both in the same year was Nadal in 2008. He faces Arthur Fery — a wildcard ranked No. 114 — on Friday. This draw. #Wimbledon #Tennis #TennisFanrecap

- [HIGH] Faces Arthur Fery on Friday SF — atptour.com
**Story angle:** Finally broke the Fritz curse. First-ever Wimbledon last four. RG-Wimbledon double now possible.

stories.title: Women's Day 10 — Kostyuk Dominates Paolini, Noskova Stuns Mertens

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Marta Kostyuk def. Jasmine Paolini 6-3, 6-2 in 69 minutes. Linda Noskova def. Elise Mertens 6-3, 7-5. Both first Wimbledon SFs. Combined with Gauff and Muchova — Wimbledon 2026 is 100% guaranteed to crown a first-time women's champion. #Wimbledon #WTA #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Noskova won 79% of her first-serve points and hit 30 winners against Mertens. Kostyuk dispatched the 2024 Wimbledon finalist in 69 minutes. The women's Final Four this year is built differently. #Wimbledon #WTA #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: Women's SF Preview — Gauff vs Muchova, Kostyuk vs Noskova Today on Centre Court

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text: Today at Wimbledon: Gauff vs Muchova on Centre Court, then Kostyuk vs Noskova. All four women chasing their first Wimbledon title. For the 10th straight year, a first-time women's champion will be crowned. Tiebreak time. #Wimbledon #WTA #TennisFanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text: Kostyuk vs Noskova: they have NEVER played each other at a Grand Slam. Both are in their first Wimbledon SF. Combined age: 43. The match no one expected, but everyone is watching. #Wimbledon #WTA #TennisFanrecap

stories.title: Men's SF Preview — Fery vs Zverev + Sinner vs Djokovic, Friday on Centre Court

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Friday at Wimbledon: Fery (No. 114 wildcard) vs Zverev (Roland Garros champion). Sinner (zero sets dropped) vs Djokovic (48 hours after a 5h15m epic, playing injured). Best day of tennis of the year. #Wimbledon #Tennis #TennisFanrecap
